concerns.text: FR-002 and US-01 Independent Test require statistical validation (p < 0.05, Cohen's d ≥ 0.5) of the Drift Score distribution. Task T018a implements this logic but is explicitly marked '[MOVED TO PHASE 4]'. Phase 3 (MVP) tasks (T013-T017) only cover scoring and export, leaving the core acceptance test for US-01 unimplemented in the phase where the story is defined as 'complete'.

US-02 Independent Test and Constitution Principle VI require validation against human-annotated ground truth with inter-annotator agreement (Kappa > 0.6). Task T021d requires ingestion of multiple annotation files but fails to specify the minimum count (3) or the Kappa threshold check logic, relying on a generic 'minimum required' check that is undefined at runtime, risking failure to meet the SC-004 success criterion.

FR-002 and Edge Cases require empty/whitespace logs to be assigned a Drift Score at 'maximum distance' (theoretically 2.0 for normalized cosine distance). Task T014 specifies assigning a 'normalized magnitude' and a 'review_flag', but lacks the explicit instruction to calculate or assign the specific maximum distance value, creating ambiguity in meeting the acceptance scenario for edge cases.

Task T005d references a specific dataset ID 'agentdog/taxonomy-v1.5' which is not defined or authorized in spec.md (which only assumes the taxonomy is 'publicly accessible'). This creates a scope risk where the implementation may fail or hallucinate a dataset name not present in the spec, requiring a spec update or task clarification.

Task T028 implements a local model (TinyLlama) for baseline comparison, contradicting spec.md US-03 Independent Test which explicitly requires a 'standard zero-shot LLM classifier' using 'gpt-4o-mini'. This mismatch means the task does not cover the specific test design defined in the spec, potentially invalidating the AUC-ROC comparison against the intended baseline.
